dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic owned the day’s AI discussion on HN: Glasswing and Mythos threads read as impressed-but-wary, with strong admiration for capability gains alongside clear safety anxiety; OpenAI and Gemini mostly appeared as weaker comparison baselines, and Copilot was absent [E1][E2][E3][E6][E7].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the strongest net vibe because it dominated front-page AI coverage with several major stories, and the comments were generally impressed by its security research, benchmark gains, and scale. The tone was not uniformly celebratory—there was substantial concern about misuse, sabotage risk, and whether such models should be publicly released—but the overall signal still landed more positive than for rivals. OpenAI and Google Gemini were mostly discussed as comparison points that Claude Mythos outperformed, while Microsoft Copilot had no meaningful signal.

openai.judgementSnippet: Mildly negative: OpenAI mostly showed up as a comparison point behind Claude Mythos, and a GLM thread also argued Codex/OpenAI lacked a moat [E6][E8].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Positive overall: Anthropic dominated HN with Glasswing/Mythos/compute threads that were impressed by capability gains and scale, though repeatedly tempered by safety and release-risk concerns plus a small anti-moat backlash [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Mildly negative: Gemini mainly appeared as a benchmark baseline trailing Claude Mythos, with little standalone enthusiasm in the provided comments [E7].

evidence.summary: Project Glasswing, an Anthropic story, drew top comments calling the system an incredible advance in vulnerability hunting and potentially transformative for software security, albeit with some caution that the post might contain marketing puffery.

The Claude Mythos Preview system-card thread highlighted very strong benchmark gains and trusted behavior while also discussing sabotage, credential theft, and why Anthropic might not release the model broadly.

The thread on assessing Claude Mythos Preview’s cybersecurity capabilities described the model as impressive but scary, with commenters debating whether the target classes were hard enough and worrying about exploit chaining at scale.

Anthropic’s compute-partnership announcement with Google and Broadcom was discussed mainly as scale/infrastructure news, with commenters focusing on power, revenue, and datacenter implications more than product praise or criticism.
In the GLM-5.1 thread, commenters said GLM produced better TypeScript than Opus and one commenter bluntly argued that Anthropic was 'absolutely cooked' and had no moat.
OpenAI appeared through GPT-5.4 in the Mythos benchmark comparison, where the numbers were presented as behind Claude Mythos on several important coding and reasoning tasks.
Google Gemini appeared through Gemini 3.1 Pro in the Mythos benchmark table, where it was mostly shown as trailing Claude Mythos and did not receive separate praise in the comments.
The GLM-5.1 thread included direct negative comparison against Codex and a broader claim that OpenAI was 'absolutely cooked' and lacked a moat.
